page: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 server family. Powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It does not include local storage or a GPU, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark results indicate that the D2ps_v5 delivers modest performance. In single-core stress-ng tests and single-threaded gzip compression, the server achieves average performance tiers. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench and stress-ng multi-core, fall into the weak and poor categories, reflecting the limitations of its dual-core configuration. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, with uncached read and write speeds categorized as weak, and small-block cached reads rated as poor. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware-accelerated AES, but the architecture lacks AVX and AMX vector extensions.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ps_v5 represents a budget-friendly entry point into ARM-based cloud computing, offering dedicated physical cores at a low resource density. Due to its modest compute and memory bandwidth, this instance is best suited for lightweight workloads. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, development and testing environments, small-scale microservices, and low-intensity background processing tasks that do not require high-performance vector instructions or massive memory throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, utilizing a 3.0 GHz Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities in stress-ng and gzip compression, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the weak to poor ranges. Lacking advanced vector extensions like AVX, this instance is a cost-effective, entry-level option for ARM development. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, including basic web serving, microservices, and development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring a 2-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor and 8.0 GB of RAM, designed for cost-efficient, lightweight workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly ARM64 virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for lightweight utility workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores at 3.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average single-core performance in stress-ng and gzip compression tests
No bundled local storage or GPU, with support for Premium Storage
Best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and ARM development
